description: Inclusion-proof verification MUST fail when the folded audit path does not reproduce the checkpoint's root_hash (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.1 step 6). This fixture takes the log-001 golden inclusion proof for leaf 0 at tree size 5 and tampers ONE element of inclusion_path (first hex nibble of element 1 flipped from '5' to '0'). The checkpoint itself is the genuine log-001 checkpoint and its signature verifies (§9.3 passes) — the failure is the tree arithmetic, not the cryptography. A verifier that accepts this proof is not verifying inclusion at all.

input.scenario: Consumer calls GET /log/proof?ctx_id=acdp://registry.example.com/12345678-1234-4321-8123-123456781234 (or receives log_inclusion on full retrieval) and independently reconstructs the leaf per RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.1 step 1 from the verified body and receipt (the rcpt-001 golden material). The served proof carries a tampered inclusion_path.

expected.behavior: Checkpoint verification (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3) passes: closed parse, signature under the registry receipt key, registry binding, form. Binding checks (§9.1 step 4) pass: tree_size 5 == checkpoint.tree_size, log_ids equal, leaf_index 0 < 5. The §9.1 step 5 fold over the tampered path yields a root ≠ checkpoint.root_hash → step 6 MUST fail. The proof MUST be reported as failed with the invalid_log_proof category; a federated resolver validating this upstream proof on a caller's behalf emits invalid_log_proof on the wire (HTTP 502, RFC-ACDP-0012 §11).
expected.verdict_independence: The body verdict (producer signature + recomputed content_hash), the RFC-ACDP-0010 registry_receipt verdict, and any RFC-ACDP-0011 lineage_head_receipt verdict are independent and may all still pass — only the log-membership evidence is invalid (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3).
